output: City officials announced Monday that the new light-rail extension will open ahead of schedule on June 15. Mayor Alvarez called it 'a rare on-time, on-budget win' and credited federal infrastructure grants for closing the funding gap. Daily ridership on the line is projected to reach 42,000 within a year.

City officials announced Monday that the new light-rail extension will open ahead of schedule on June 15. Mayor Alvarez called it 'a rare on-time, on-budget win' and credited federal infrastructure grants for closing the funding gap. Daily ridership on the line is projected to reach 42,000 within a year.

City officials revealed that the light-rail extension will launch earlier than planned on June 15, instead of August, due to the contractor finishing track-laying ahead of schedule. Mayor Alvarez praised the project as a successful achievement, attributing its funding to federal infrastructure grants. It is estimated that the line will attract 42,000 daily riders within a year.

A magnitude 5.8 earthquake struck off the coast of northern Chile early Tuesday, the U.S. Geological Survey reported. Power was knocked out in three coastal towns and at least 12 buildings sustained structural damage. Local officials said no fatalities had been reported as of midday.

Pharma giant Norvelt said Wednesday it will halt development of its experimental Alzheimer's drug after a Phase 3 trial showed no benefit over placebo. The company's stock fell 14% in pre-market trading. Analysts had expected the drug to generate $2 billion in annual sales by 2028.

The Meteorological Department predicts another 100 mm of rain by Friday. Schools in five districts are closed and several subway stations have suspended service.

Brazil's senate approved a sweeping tax reform bill late Wednesday by a vote of 53 to 22. President Lula praised the bill as 'the most significant economic reform in three decades.' Critics warn the transition period will create accounting confusion for small businesses.

Scientists at the Large Hadron Collider announced preliminary evidence of an unexpectedly heavy variant of the W boson, potentially in tension with the Standard Model. The result, presented at a Geneva conference, carries a statistical significance of 3.4 sigma — below the 5-sigma threshold for discovery. Theorists are already proposing extensions to accommodate the result.

South Korea's central bank cut its benchmark rate by 25 basis points to 3.00% on Thursday, citing weakening export demand. The decision was unanimous among the seven-member board. The bank also revised its 2026 GDP growth forecast down to 1.9% from 2.2%.

A new exhibition at Tate Modern showcases 80 works by South Korean artist Lee Bul, highlighting her cyborg sculptures and large architectural installations over the past 40 years. Curator Achim Borchardt-Hume praised Lee as a leading contemporary sculptor. The exhibition will be open until January.
